site.title: The Misread Machine
site.description: An interactive thought-tool about expertise, trust, and how good work gets misread online.

hero.headline: Expertise can be deep and still be misread in seconds.
hero.subhead: A concept-led lab for testing how language shifts trust, clarity, and buyer interpretation.

premise.headline: Why this exists
paragraphs: Smart people often get flattened online. Nuance gets compressed into safe claims, clean frameworks, and generic language.
The result is a trust gap: your expertise may be real, but buyers cannot read it quickly.
This machine explores the gap between intent and interpretation, and how better signal design closes it.

items.title: Specificity
items.body: Name who, what, and outcome. General claims feel interchangeable.

items.title: Proof
items.body: Evidence beats assertion. Light proof often outperforms heavy rhetoric.

items.title: Clarity
items.body: If buyers must decode your point, they usually move on.

items.title: Tension
items.body: Useful contrast shows judgment. Flat consensus sounds generic.

items.title: Warmth
items.body: Credibility lands faster when it feels human, not performative.

closing.headline: Most experts do not need more content.
closing.body: They need language that makes their depth legible to people who are scanning fast.
